seoTitle: Fitzgerald Creek vs Fitzroy Island — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Fitzgerald Creek and Fitzroy Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Fitzgerald Creek vs Fitzroy Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Fitzgerald Creek and Fitzroy Island.
heading: Fitzgerald Creek vs Fitzroy Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Fitzgerald Creek, Queensland and Fitzroy Island, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Fitzroy Island has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Fitzgerald Creek (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Fitzgerald Creek or Fitzroy Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Fitzgerald Creek has a flood risk score of 42/100, while Fitzroy Island scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Fitzgerald Creek scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Fitzroy Island scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Fitzgerald Creek or Fitzroy Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Fitzroy Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
